column4: Validate that the per-user monthly quota of 10 solutions is atomically enforced and the 11th submission is rejected with HTTP 429
column5: Staging environment with quota enforcement enabled; test user seeded with quotaUsed=9 via admin reset
column6:
1. Submit solution 10 for the test user — expect success
2. Immediately submit solution 11 for the same user
3. Verify Solution Create Lambda conditional write fails on attempt 11
4. Confirm DynamoDB User record shows quotaUsed=10 and has not been incremented beyond 10
5. Verify no Bedrock AgentCore invocation was made for the rejected request
column7: HTTP 429 Too Many Requests for submission 11; response body contains quota exceeded message; DynamoDB User.quotaUsed remains at 10; zero Bedrock spend for the rejected attempt
column13: DD §4 Component Design — Solution Create Lambda; SOW §Scope of Work — per-user quota 10 solutions/month; IG §Integration Testing — Quota Enforcement
column14: Confirm atomic enforcement under concurrency with FT-010

column4: Validate that the global monthly quota of 1000 solutions is atomically enforced and no bypass occurs under concurrent load

1. Simultaneously submit 5 solution generation requests from 5 different test users
2. Observe which requests succeed and which receive HTTP 429
3. Verify GlobalQuota.solutionsGenerated does not exceed 1000
4. Confirm exactly 1 of the 5 concurrent submissions succeeds
5. Confirm remaining 4 requests receive HTTP 429
column7: Exactly one submission succeeds (1000th solution); four submissions receive HTTP 429; GlobalQuota.solutionsGenerated remains at exactly 1000; no Bedrock spend for the 4 rejected requests
column13: DD §4 Component Design — DynamoDB Quota Enforcement; SOW §Scope of Work — global quota 1000/month; IG §Integration Testing — Quota Enforcement
column14: Zero bypass events required — critical acceptance gate

column4: Validate that the EO Validator Agent attempts up to 3 validation cycles and correctly flags an artifact as VALIDATION_FAILED if all three attempts fail

2. Observe the EO Validator Agent log for retry attempt 1 — verify error envelope returned
3. Confirm retry attempt 2 is made with the re-generated artifact
4. Confirm retry attempt 3 is made
5. After the third failure verify DynamoDB Solution record updated to status=VALIDATION_FAILED
6. Confirm the artifact is NOT committed to GitHub
column7: EO Validator makes exactly 3 attempts; DynamoDB Solution record transitions to VALIDATION_FAILED after the third failed attempt; no GitHub commit is made; CloudWatch log entry captures the error envelope for all three attempts

column3: Lambda Cold-Start Latency — Solution Create Function
column4: Validate that Solution Create Lambda p99 cold-start latency is acceptable and Provisioned Concurrency eliminates cold-start for the two warm instances in production
column5: Production-equivalent staging configuration; Provisioned Concurrency set to 2 instances on Solution Create Lambda; CloudWatch X-Ray tracing enabled
column6:
1. Invoke the Solution Create Lambda 50 times over 10 minutes with valid JWT and well-formed brief payload
2. Collect AWS X-Ray trace data for all 50 invocations
3. Distinguish cold-start from warm invocations in trace data
4. Calculate p50/p95/p99 latency for the Lambda handler excluding AgentCore invocation time
5. Verify Provisioned Concurrency removes cold-start penalty for concurrent requests
column7: Solution Create Lambda handler latency: p95 under 2 seconds; p99 under 5 seconds; provisioned concurrency instances show zero cold-start duration; CLI auth login completes in under 5 seconds end-to-end
column13: DD §9 Infrastructure — Compute Sizing (Solution Create Lambda 512MB 30s timeout); IG §Infrastructure Deployment — Compute; SOW §Testing — Performance Testing
column14: Configure 2 Provisioned Concurrency instances per IG §Compute Deployment Steps

column3: JWT Bypass Testing — All Attack Vectors
column4: Validate that the platform cannot be accessed with expired tokens, wrong-key-signed tokens, manipulated sub/scope claims, or missing Authorization headers
column5: Production-equivalent security configuration; API Gateway JWT authoriser configured; test tokens prepared: expired token and wrong RSA key token and manipulated sub field token and invalid iss claim token
column6:
1. Send GET /v1/quota with no Authorization header
2. Send GET /v1/quota with a JWT expired 2 hours ago
3. Send POST /v1/solution with a JWT signed by an incorrect RSA key pair
4. Send GET /v1/user/profile with a JWT whose sub claim has been altered
5. Send GET /v1/admin/usage with a consultant-group JWT (not admin group)
6. Send a request with a JWT whose iss claim does not match the Cognito User Pool
column7: All six attempts receive 401 Unauthorized for cases 1 through 4 or 403 Forbidden for case 5; no Lambda function invocation triggered for any rejected request; WAF custom rule blocks invalid iss claim before reaching JWT authoriser; all blocked attempts logged in CloudTrail
column13: DD §5 Security — Identity and Access Management; SOW §Testing — Security Testing — JWT bypass; IG §Security Validation — Phase 2 Security Quality Gate
column14: OWASP A01; required Phase 2 security gate per IG

column3: WAF Rule Validation — SQLi and XSS Payloads
column4: Validate that the AWS WAF WebACL blocks SQL injection and XSS payloads and IP-based rate limiting fires at 101 requests per minute
column5: WAF WebACL attached to API Gateway stage; AWS Managed Rules Common Rule Set enabled; CloudWatch WAF logging active
column6:
1. Submit a POST /v1/solution request body containing a SQLi payload
2. Submit a GET /v1/solutions request with a URL query parameter containing an XSS payload
3. Submit 101 requests from the same IP address within 1 minute
4. Verify all three attack types are blocked
5. Confirm WAF BlockedRequests CloudWatch metric increments for each blocked request
column7: HTTP 403 for SQLi payload request; HTTP 403 for XSS payload request; HTTP 429 for the 101st request from the rate-limited IP; WAF CloudWatch BlockedRequests metric shows correct count; WAF events logged to CloudWatch Logs
column13: DD §5 Security — Network Security (WAF); SOW §Security — Monitoring and Threat Detection; IG §Security Validation — Phase 2 Quality Gate
column14: AWS Managed Rules CRS covers OWASP A03 SQLi and XSS

column3: IAM Least-Privilege Validation — No Wildcard ARNs

3. Verify Pre-Sales Generator Lambda role has bedrock:InvokeModel scoped to Sonnet 4.6 ARN only
4. Verify GitHub Integration Lambda role can only call secretsmanager:GetSecretValue on the github/pat secret ARN
5. Verify a Lambda function outside the approved set cannot retrieve the GitHub PAT
column7: IAM Access Analyzer reports zero active findings for all 12 Lambda execution roles; zero wildcard resource ARNs in any Lambda policy; Pre-Sales Generator Lambda cannot invoke Haiku 4.5 model; GitHub Lambda cannot access DynamoDB tables

column4: Validate that DynamoDB PITR can restore the Solutions table to a specific point-in-time snapshot within the 2-hour RTO target
